question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential execution of cleanup() before SpringApplication.run() to inject files into /tmp/web3 during the window between Files.createDirectories(tmpDir) and the first EVM simulation, causing the simulation to load attacker-controlled state that bypasses gossip-prevention checks?
